title: Oritrophium ferrugineum
taxonomy: Oritrophium ferrugineum is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Oritrophium, which is a group of flowering plants that are native to South America. The family Asteraceae, also known as the daisy or sunflower family, is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that are found all over the world. This plant's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the Asteraceae family. Further classification of Oritrophium ferrugineum may include its order, class, and kingdom.
uses: Oritrophium ferrugineum, also known as the coppery orris, has been reported to have medicinal properties. Its roots, leaves, and stems are used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Traditional healers continue to use Oritrophium ferrugineum for its perceived medicinal benefits.
